items.title: Tracking Jeremy Grantham's GMO Capital Portfolio - Q4 2025 Update
Warren Buffett Retires With a $187 Billion Warning to Investors. History Says the Stock Market Will Do This Next.
items.author: Trevor Jennewine
items.description: Warren Buffett's retirement marks a significant warning to investors as Berkshire Hathaway has been a net seller of stocks for 13 consecutive quarters, totaling $187 billion in sales. With the S&P 500's CAPE ratio at 39.8 in February 2026—the highest since the dot-com crash—historical data suggests the index could decline by 30% over the next three years if valuations don't improve or earnings don't grow substantially.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Berkshire initiated a position in Alphabet despite the broader market concerns, suggesting selective confidence in this company despite overall market caution.
Berkshire initiated a position in Alphabet despite the broader market concerns, suggesting selective confidence in this company despite overall market caution.
Berkshire started a position in UnitedHealth Group, indicating selective investment despite the company's overall net-selling stance in the market.
Berkshire initiated a position in The New York Times, showing selective buying despite broader market valuation concerns.

Berkshire has been a net seller of stocks for 13 straight quarters with $187 billion in sales, indicating management's concern about elevated market valuations and difficulty finding reasonably priced investments.
